evidence.claim: ’Tis a form of government which the word of God bears testimony against, and blood will attend it.

candidate_claims.statement: ’Tis a form of government which the word of God bears testimony against, and blood will attend it.

evidence.claim: If we enquire into the business of a King, we shall find that in some countries they may have none; and after sauntering away their lives without pleasure to themselves or advantage to the nation, withdraw from the scene, and leave their successors to tread the same idle round.

candidate_claims.statement: If we enquire into the business of a King, we shall find that in some countries they may have none; and after sauntering away their lives without pleasure to themselves or advantage to the nation, withdraw from the scene, and leave their successors to tread the same idle round.

evidence.claim: In absolute monarchies the whole weight of business civil and military lies on the King; the children of Israel in their request for a king urged this plea, “that he may judge us, and go out before us and fight our battles.” But in countries where he is neither a Judge nor a General, as in England, a man would be puzzled to know what _is_ his business.

candidate_claims.statement: In absolute monarchies the whole weight of business civil and military lies on the King; the children of Israel in their request for a king urged this plea, “that he may judge us, and go out before us and fight our battles.” But in countries where he is neither a Judge nor a General, as in England, a man would be puzzled to know what _is_ his business.

evidence.claim: The nearer any government approaches to a Republic, the less business there is for a King.

candidate_claims.statement: The nearer any government approaches to a Republic, the less business there is for a King.

evidence.claim: It is somewhat difficult to find a proper name for the government of England.

candidate_claims.statement: It is somewhat difficult to find a proper name for the government of England.

evidence.claim: Sir William Meredith calls it a Republic; but in its present state it is unworthy of the name, because the corrupt influence of the Crown, by having all the places in its disposal, hath so effectually swallowed up the power, and eaten out the virtue of the House of Commons (the Republican part in the constitution) that the government of England is nearly as monarchical as that of France or Spain.

candidate_claims.statement: Sir William Meredith calls it a Republic; but in its present state it is unworthy of the name, because the corrupt influence of the Crown, by having all the places in its disposal, hath so effectually swallowed up the power, and eaten out the virtue of the House of Commons (the Republican part in the constitution) that the government of England is nearly as monarchical as that of France or Spain.

evidence.claim: Men fall out with names without understanding them.

candidate_claims.statement: Men fall out with names without understanding them.

evidence.claim: For ’tis the Republican and not the Monarchical part of the Constitution of England which Englishmen glory in, viz.

candidate_claims.statement: For ’tis the Republican and not the Monarchical part of the Constitution of England which Englishmen glory in, viz.

evidence.claim: the liberty of choosing an House of Commons from out of their own body—and it is easy to see that when Republican virtues fail, slavery ensues.

candidate_claims.statement: the liberty of choosing an House of Commons from out of their own body—and it is easy to see that when Republican virtues fail, slavery ensues.

evidence.claim: Why is the constitution of England sickly, but because monarchy hath poisoned the Republic; the Crown hath engrossed the Commons.

candidate_claims.statement: Why is the constitution of England sickly, but because monarchy hath poisoned the Republic; the Crown hath engrossed the Commons.

evidence.claim: In England a King hath little more to do than to make war and giveaway places; which, in plain terms, is to empoverish the nation and set it together by the ears.

candidate_claims.statement: In England a King hath little more to do than to make war and giveaway places; which, in plain terms, is to empoverish the nation and set it together by the ears.

evidence.claim: A pretty business indeed for a man to be allowed eight hundred thousand sterling a year for, and worshipped into the bargain!

candidate_claims.statement: A pretty business indeed for a man to be allowed eight hundred thousand sterling a year for, and worshipped into the bargain!

evidence.claim: Of more worth is one honest man to society, and in the sight of God, than all the crowned ruffians that ever lived.

candidate_claims.statement: Of more worth is one honest man to society, and in the sight of God, than all the crowned ruffians that ever lived.

evidence.claim: Thoughts on the Present State of American Affairs

candidate_claims.statement: Thoughts on the Present State of American Affairs

evidence.claim: In the following pages I offer nothing more than simple facts, plain arguments, and common sense: and have no other preliminaries to settle with the reader, than that he will divest himself of prejudice and prepossession, and suffer his reason and his feelings to determine for themselves that he will put on, or rather that he will not put off, the true character of a man, and generously enlarge his views beyond the present day.

candidate_claims.statement: In the following pages I offer nothing more than simple facts, plain arguments, and common sense: and have no other preliminaries to settle with the reader, than that he will divest himself of prejudice and prepossession, and suffer his reason and his feelings to determine for themselves that he will put on, or rather that he will not put off, the true character of a man, and generously enlarge his views beyond the present day.

evidence.claim: Volumes have been written on the subject of the struggle between England and America.

candidate_claims.statement: Volumes have been written on the subject of the struggle between England and America.

evidence.claim: Men of all ranks have embarked in the controversy, from different motives, and with various designs; but all have been ineffectual, and the period of debate is closed.

candidate_claims.statement: Men of all ranks have embarked in the controversy, from different motives, and with various designs; but all have been ineffectual, and the period of debate is closed.

evidence.claim: Arms as the last resource decide the contest; the appeal was the choice of the King, and the Continent has accepted the challenge.

candidate_claims.statement: Arms as the last resource decide the contest; the appeal was the choice of the King, and the Continent has accepted the challenge.

evidence.claim: It hath been reported of the late Mr.

candidate_claims.statement: It hath been reported of the late Mr.

evidence.claim: Pelham (who tho’ an able minister was not without his faults) that on his being attacked in the House of Commons on the score that his measures were only of a temporary kind, replied, _“they will last my time.”_ Should a thought so fatal and unmanly possess the Colonies in the present contest, the name of ancestors will be remembered by future generations with detestation.

candidate_claims.statement: Pelham (who tho’ an able minister was not without his faults) that on his being attacked in the House of Commons on the score that his measures were only of a temporary kind, replied, _“they will last my time.”_ Should a thought so fatal and unmanly possess the Colonies in the present contest, the name of ancestors will be remembered by future generations with detestation.

evidence.claim: The Sun never shined on a cause of greater worth.

candidate_claims.statement: The Sun never shined on a cause of greater worth.

evidence.claim: ’Tis not the affair of a City, a County, a Province, or a Kingdom; but of a Continent—of at least one-eighth part of the habitable Globe.

candidate_claims.statement: ’Tis not the affair of a City, a County, a Province, or a Kingdom; but of a Continent—of at least one-eighth part of the habitable Globe.

evidence.claim: ’Tis not the concern of a day, a year, or an age; posterity are virtually involved in the contest, and will be more or less affected even to the end of time, by the proceedings now.

candidate_claims.statement: ’Tis not the concern of a day, a year, or an age; posterity are virtually involved in the contest, and will be more or less affected even to the end of time, by the proceedings now.

evidence.claim: Now is the seed-time of Continental union, faith and honour.

candidate_claims.statement: Now is the seed-time of Continental union, faith and honour.

evidence.claim: The least fracture now will be like a name engraved with the point of a pin on the tender rind of a young oak; the wound would enlarge with the tree, and posterity read in it full grown characters.

candidate_claims.statement: The least fracture now will be like a name engraved with the point of a pin on the tender rind of a young oak; the wound would enlarge with the tree, and posterity read in it full grown characters.

evidence.claim: By referring the matter from argument to arms, a new era for politics is struck—a new method of thinking hath arisen.

candidate_claims.statement: By referring the matter from argument to arms, a new era for politics is struck—a new method of thinking hath arisen.

evidence.claim: All plans, proposals, &c.

candidate_claims.statement: All plans, proposals, &c.

evidence.claim: prior to the nineteenth of April, i.e.

candidate_claims.statement: prior to the nineteenth of April, i.e.

evidence.claim: to the commencement of hostilities, are like the almanacks of the last year; which tho’ proper then, are superceded and useless now.

candidate_claims.statement: to the commencement of hostilities, are like the almanacks of the last year; which tho’ proper then, are superceded and useless now.

evidence.claim: Whatever was advanced by the advocates on either side of the question then, terminated in one and the same point, viz.

candidate_claims.statement: Whatever was advanced by the advocates on either side of the question then, terminated in one and the same point, viz.

evidence.claim: a union with Great Britain; the only difference between the parties was the method of effecting it; the one proposing force, the other friendship; but it hath so far happened that the first hath failed, and the second hath withdrawn her influence.

candidate_claims.statement: a union with Great Britain; the only difference between the parties was the method of effecting it; the one proposing force, the other friendship; but it hath so far happened that the first hath failed, and the second hath withdrawn her influence.

evidence.claim: As much hath been said of the advantages of reconciliation, which, like an agreeable dream, hath passed away and left us as we were, it is but right that we should examine the contrary side of the argument, and enquire into some of the many material injuries which these Colonies sustain, and always will sustain, by being connected with and dependant on Great Britain.

candidate_claims.statement: As much hath been said of the advantages of reconciliation, which, like an agreeable dream, hath passed away and left us as we were, it is but right that we should examine the contrary side of the argument, and enquire into some of the many material injuries which these Colonies sustain, and always will sustain, by being connected with and dependant on Great Britain.

evidence.claim: To examine that connection and dependance, on the principles of nature and common sense, to see what we have to trust to, if separated, and what we are to expect, if dependant.

candidate_claims.statement: To examine that connection and dependance, on the principles of nature and common sense, to see what we have to trust to, if separated, and what we are to expect, if dependant.

evidence.claim: I have heard it asserted by some, that as America has flourished under her former connection with Great Britain, the same connection is necessary towards her future happiness, and will always have the same effect.

candidate_claims.statement: I have heard it asserted by some, that as America has flourished under her former connection with Great Britain, the same connection is necessary towards her future happiness, and will always have the same effect.

evidence.claim: Nothing can be more fallacious than this kind of argument.

candidate_claims.statement: Nothing can be more fallacious than this kind of argument.

evidence.claim: We may as well assert that because a child has thrived upon milk, that it is never to have meat, or that the first twenty years of our lives is to become a precedent for the next twenty.

candidate_claims.statement: We may as well assert that because a child has thrived upon milk, that it is never to have meat, or that the first twenty years of our lives is to become a precedent for the next twenty.

evidence.claim: But even this is admitting more than is true; for I answer roundly that America would have flourished as much, and probably much more, had no European power taken any notice of her.

candidate_claims.statement: But even this is admitting more than is true; for I answer roundly that America would have flourished as much, and probably much more, had no European power taken any notice of her.

evidence.claim: The commerce by which she hath enriched herself are the necessaries of life, and will always have a market while eating is the custom of Europe.

candidate_claims.statement: The commerce by which she hath enriched herself are the necessaries of life, and will always have a market while eating is the custom of Europe.

evidence.claim: But she has protected us, say some.

candidate_claims.statement: But she has protected us, say some.

evidence.claim: That she hath engrossed us is true, and defended the Continent at our expense as well as her own, is admitted; and she would have defended Turkey from the same motive, viz.—for the sake of trade and dominion.

candidate_claims.statement: That she hath engrossed us is true, and defended the Continent at our expense as well as her own, is admitted; and she would have defended Turkey from the same motive, viz.—for the sake of trade and dominion.

evidence.claim: we have been long led away by ancient prejudices and made large sacrifices to superstition.

candidate_claims.statement: we have been long led away by ancient prejudices and made large sacrifices to superstition.

evidence.claim: We have boasted the protection of Great Britain, without considering, that her motive was _interest_ not _attachment_ ; and that she did not protect us from _our enemies_ on _our account_ ; but from _her enemies_ on _her own account_ , from those who had no quarrel with us on any _other account_ , and who will always be our enemies on the _same account_ .

candidate_claims.statement: We have boasted the protection of Great Britain, without considering, that her motive was _interest_ not _attachment_ ; and that she did not protect us from _our enemies_ on _our account_ ; but from _her enemies_ on _her own account_ , from those who had no quarrel with us on any _other account_ , and who will always be our enemies on the _same account_ .

evidence.claim: Let Britain waive her pretensions to the Continent, or the Continent throw off the dependance, and we should be at peace with France and Spain, were they at war with Britain.

candidate_claims.statement: Let Britain waive her pretensions to the Continent, or the Continent throw off the dependance, and we should be at peace with France and Spain, were they at war with Britain.

evidence.claim: The miseries of Hanover last war ought to warn us against connections.

candidate_claims.statement: The miseries of Hanover last war ought to warn us against connections.

evidence.claim: It hath lately been asserted in parliament, that the Colonies have no relation to each other but through the Parent Country, i.e.

candidate_claims.statement: It hath lately been asserted in parliament, that the Colonies have no relation to each other but through the Parent Country, i.e.

evidence.claim: that Pennsylvania and the Jerseys and so on for the rest, are sister Colonies by the way of England; this is certainly a very roundabout way of proving relationship, but it is the nearest and only true way of proving enmity (or enemyship, if I may so call it.) France and Spain never were, nor perhaps ever will be, our enemies as _Americans_ , but as our being the _subjects of Great Britain_ .

candidate_claims.statement: that Pennsylvania and the Jerseys and so on for the rest, are sister Colonies by the way of England; this is certainly a very roundabout way of proving relationship, but it is the nearest and only true way of proving enmity (or enemyship, if I may so call it.) France and Spain never were, nor perhaps ever will be, our enemies as _Americans_ , but as our being the _subjects of Great Britain_ .

evidence.claim: But Britain is the parent country, say some.

candidate_claims.statement: But Britain is the parent country, say some.

evidence.claim: Then the more shame upon her conduct.

candidate_claims.statement: Then the more shame upon her conduct.

evidence.claim: Even brutes do not devour their young, nor savages make war upon their families.

candidate_claims.statement: Even brutes do not devour their young, nor savages make war upon their families.

evidence.claim: Wherefore, the assertion, if true, turns to her reproach; but it happens not to be true, or only partly so, and the phrase _parent or mother country_ hath been jesuitically adopted by the King and his parasites, with a low papistical design of gaining an unfair bias on the credulous weakness of our minds.

candidate_claims.statement: Wherefore, the assertion, if true, turns to her reproach; but it happens not to be true, or only partly so, and the phrase _parent or mother country_ hath been jesuitically adopted by the King and his parasites, with a low papistical design of gaining an unfair bias on the credulous weakness of our minds.

evidence.claim: Europe, and not England, is the parent country of America.

candidate_claims.statement: Europe, and not England, is the parent country of America.

evidence.claim: This new World hath been the asylum for the persecuted lovers of civil and religious liberty from _every part_ of Europe.

candidate_claims.statement: This new World hath been the asylum for the persecuted lovers of civil and religious liberty from _every part_ of Europe.

evidence.claim: Hither have they fled, not from the tender embraces of the mother, but from the cruelty of the monster; and it is so far true of England, that the same tyranny which drove the first emigrants from home, pursues their descendants still.

candidate_claims.statement: Hither have they fled, not from the tender embraces of the mother, but from the cruelty of the monster; and it is so far true of England, that the same tyranny which drove the first emigrants from home, pursues their descendants still.

evidence.claim: In this extensive quarter of the globe, we forget the narrow limits of three hundred and sixty miles (the extent of England) and carry our friendship on a larger scale; we claim brotherhood with every European Christian, and triumph in the generosity of the sentiment.

candidate_claims.statement: In this extensive quarter of the globe, we forget the narrow limits of three hundred and sixty miles (the extent of England) and carry our friendship on a larger scale; we claim brotherhood with every European Christian, and triumph in the generosity of the sentiment.

evidence.claim: It is pleasant to observe by what regular gradations we surmount the force of local prejudices, as we enlarge our acquaintance with the World.

candidate_claims.statement: It is pleasant to observe by what regular gradations we surmount the force of local prejudices, as we enlarge our acquaintance with the World.

evidence.claim: A man born in any town in England divided into parishes, will naturally associate most with his fellow parishioners (because their interests in many cases will be common) and distinguish him by the name of _neighbor_ ; if he meet him but a few miles from home, he drops the narrow idea of a street, and salutes him by the name of _townsman_ ; if he travel out of the county and meet him in any other, he forgets the minor divisions of street and town, and calls him _countryman_ , i.e.

candidate_claims.statement: A man born in any town in England divided into parishes, will naturally associate most with his fellow parishioners (because their interests in many cases will be common) and distinguish him by the name of _neighbor_ ; if he meet him but a few miles from home, he drops the narrow idea of a street, and salutes him by the name of _townsman_ ; if he travel out of the county and meet him in any other, he forgets the minor divisions of street and town, and calls him _countryman_ , i.e.

evidence.claim: _countyman_ ; but if in their foreign excursions they should associate in France, or any other part of _Europe_ , their local remembrance would be enlarged into that of _Englishmen_ .

candidate_claims.statement: _countyman_ ; but if in their foreign excursions they should associate in France, or any other part of _Europe_ , their local remembrance would be enlarged into that of _Englishmen_ .

evidence.claim: And by a just parity of reasoning, all Europeans meeting in America, or any other quarter of the globe, are _countrymen_ ; for England, Holland, Germany, or Sweden, when compared with the whole, stand in the same places on the larger scale, which the divisions of street, town, and county do on the smaller ones; Distinctions too limited for Continental minds.

candidate_claims.statement: And by a just parity of reasoning, all Europeans meeting in America, or any other quarter of the globe, are _countrymen_ ; for England, Holland, Germany, or Sweden, when compared with the whole, stand in the same places on the larger scale, which the divisions of street, town, and county do on the smaller ones; Distinctions too limited for Continental minds.

evidence.claim: Not one third of the inhabitants, even of this province, [Pennsylvania], are of English descent.

candidate_claims.statement: Not one third of the inhabitants, even of this province, [Pennsylvania], are of English descent.

evidence.claim: reconciliation is our duty, is truly farcical.

candidate_claims.statement: reconciliation is our duty, is truly farcical.

evidence.claim: The first king of England, of the present line (William the Conqueror) was a Frenchman, and half the peers of England are descendants from the same country; wherefore, by the same method of reasoning, England ought to be governed by France.

candidate_claims.statement: The first king of England, of the present line (William the Conqueror) was a Frenchman, and half the peers of England are descendants from the same country; wherefore, by the same method of reasoning, England ought to be governed by France.

evidence.claim: Much hath been said of the united strength of Britain and the Colonies, that in conjunction they might bid defiance to the world.

candidate_claims.statement: Much hath been said of the united strength of Britain and the Colonies, that in conjunction they might bid defiance to the world.

evidence.claim: But this is mere presumption; the fate of war is uncertain, neither do the expressions mean anything; for this continent would never suffer itself to be drained of inhabitants, to support the British arms in either Asia, Africa, or Europe.

candidate_claims.statement: But this is mere presumption; the fate of war is uncertain, neither do the expressions mean anything; for this continent would never suffer itself to be drained of inhabitants, to support the British arms in either Asia, Africa, or Europe.

evidence.claim: Besides, what have we to do with setting the world at defiance?

candidate_claims.statement: Besides, what have we to do with setting the world at defiance?

evidence.claim: Our plan is commerce, and that, well attended to, will secure us the peace and friendship of all Europe; because it is the interest of all Europe to have America a free port.

candidate_claims.statement: Our plan is commerce, and that, well attended to, will secure us the peace and friendship of all Europe; because it is the interest of all Europe to have America a free port.

evidence.claim: Her trade will always be a protection, and her barrenness of gold and silver secure her from invaders.

candidate_claims.statement: Her trade will always be a protection, and her barrenness of gold and silver secure her from invaders.

evidence.claim: I challenge the warmest advocate for reconciliation to show a single advantage that this continent can reap by being connected with Great Britain.

candidate_claims.statement: I challenge the warmest advocate for reconciliation to show a single advantage that this continent can reap by being connected with Great Britain.

evidence.claim: I repeat the challenge; not a single advantage is derived.

candidate_claims.statement: I repeat the challenge; not a single advantage is derived.

evidence.claim: Our corn will fetch its price in any market in Europe, and our imported goods must be paid for buy them where we will.

candidate_claims.statement: Our corn will fetch its price in any market in Europe, and our imported goods must be paid for buy them where we will.

evidence.claim: But the injuries and disadvantages which we sustain by that connection, are without number; and our duty to mankind at large, as well as to ourselves, instruct us to renounce the alliance: because, any submission to, or dependance on, Great Britain, tends directly to involve this Continent in European wars and quarrels, and set us at variance with nations who would otherwise seek our friendship, and against whom we have neither anger nor complaint.

candidate_claims.statement: But the injuries and disadvantages which we sustain by that connection, are without number; and our duty to mankind at large, as well as to ourselves, instruct us to renounce the alliance: because, any submission to, or dependance on, Great Britain, tends directly to involve this Continent in European wars and quarrels, and set us at variance with nations who would otherwise seek our friendship, and against whom we have neither anger nor complaint.

evidence.claim: As Europe is our market for trade, we ought to form no partial connection with any part of it.

candidate_claims.statement: As Europe is our market for trade, we ought to form no partial connection with any part of it.

evidence.claim: It is the true interest of America to steer clear of European contentions, which she never can do, while, by her dependance on Britain, she is made the makeweight in the scale of British politics.

candidate_claims.statement: It is the true interest of America to steer clear of European contentions, which she never can do, while, by her dependance on Britain, she is made the makeweight in the scale of British politics.

evidence.claim: Europe is too thickly planted with Kingdoms to be long at peace, and whenever a war breaks out between England and any foreign power, the trade of America goes to ruin, _because of her connection with Britain_ .

candidate_claims.statement: Europe is too thickly planted with Kingdoms to be long at peace, and whenever a war breaks out between England and any foreign power, the trade of America goes to ruin, _because of her connection with Britain_ .

evidence.claim: The next war may not turn out like the last, and should it not, the advocates for reconciliation now will be wishing for separation then, because neutrality in that case would be a safer convoy than a man of war.

candidate_claims.statement: The next war may not turn out like the last, and should it not, the advocates for reconciliation now will be wishing for separation then, because neutrality in that case would be a safer convoy than a man of war.

evidence.claim: Every thing that is right or reasonable pleads for separation.

candidate_claims.statement: Every thing that is right or reasonable pleads for separation.

evidence.claim: The blood of the slain, the weeping voice of nature cries, _’tis time to part_ .

candidate_claims.statement: The blood of the slain, the weeping voice of nature cries, _’tis time to part_ .

evidence.claim: Even the distance at which the Almighty hath placed England and America is a strong and natural proof that the authority of the one over the other, was never the design of Heaven.

candidate_claims.statement: Even the distance at which the Almighty hath placed England and America is a strong and natural proof that the authority of the one over the other, was never the design of Heaven.

evidence.claim: The time likewise at which the Continent was discovered, adds weight to the argument, and the manner in which it was peopled, encreases the force of it.

candidate_claims.statement: The time likewise at which the Continent was discovered, adds weight to the argument, and the manner in which it was peopled, encreases the force of it.

evidence.claim: The Reformation was preceded by the discovery of America: As if the Almighty graciously meant to open a sanctuary to the persecuted in future years, when home should afford neither friendship nor safety.

candidate_claims.statement: The Reformation was preceded by the discovery of America: As if the Almighty graciously meant to open a sanctuary to the persecuted in future years, when home should afford neither friendship nor safety.

evidence.claim: The authority of Great Britain over this continent, is a form of government, which sooner or later must have an end: And a serious mind can draw no true pleasure by looking forward, under the painful and positive conviction that what he calls “the present constitution” is merely temporary.

candidate_claims.statement: The authority of Great Britain over this continent, is a form of government, which sooner or later must have an end: And a serious mind can draw no true pleasure by looking forward, under the painful and positive conviction that what he calls “the present constitution” is merely temporary.

evidence.claim: As parents, we can have no joy, knowing that this government is not sufficiently lasting to ensure any thing which we may bequeath to posterity: And by a plain method of argument, as we are running the next generation into debt, we ought to do the work of it, otherwise we use them meanly and pitifully.

candidate_claims.statement: As parents, we can have no joy, knowing that this government is not sufficiently lasting to ensure any thing which we may bequeath to posterity: And by a plain method of argument, as we are running the next generation into debt, we ought to do the work of it, otherwise we use them meanly and pitifully.

evidence.claim: In order to discover the line of our duty rightly, we should take our children in our hand, and fix our station a few years farther into life; that eminence will present a prospect which a few present fears and prejudices conceal from our sight.

candidate_claims.statement: In order to discover the line of our duty rightly, we should take our children in our hand, and fix our station a few years farther into life; that eminence will present a prospect which a few present fears and prejudices conceal from our sight.

evidence.claim: Though I would carefully avoid giving unnecessary offence, yet I am inclined to believe, that all those who espouse the doctrine of reconciliation, may be included within the following descriptions.

candidate_claims.statement: Though I would carefully avoid giving unnecessary offence, yet I am inclined to believe, that all those who espouse the doctrine of reconciliation, may be included within the following descriptions.

evidence.claim: Interested men, who are not to be trusted, weak men who _cannot_ see,

candidate_claims.statement: Interested men, who are not to be trusted, weak men who _cannot_ see,

evidence.claim: prejudiced men who will not see, and a certain set of moderate men who think better of the European world than it deserves; and this last class, by an ill-judged deliberation, will be the cause of more calamities to this Continent than all the other three.

candidate_claims.statement: prejudiced men who will not see, and a certain set of moderate men who think better of the European world than it deserves; and this last class, by an ill-judged deliberation, will be the cause of more calamities to this Continent than all the other three.

evidence.claim: It is the good fortune of many to live distant from the scene of present sorrow; the evil is not sufficiently brought to their doors to make them feel the precariousness with which all American property is possessed.

candidate_claims.statement: It is the good fortune of many to live distant from the scene of present sorrow; the evil is not sufficiently brought to their doors to make them feel the precariousness with which all American property is possessed.

evidence.claim: But let our imaginations transport us a few moments to Boston; that seat of wretchedness will teach us wisdom, and instruct us for ever to renounce a power in whom we can have no trust.

candidate_claims.statement: But let our imaginations transport us a few moments to Boston; that seat of wretchedness will teach us wisdom, and instruct us for ever to renounce a power in whom we can have no trust.

evidence.claim: The inhabitants of that unfortunate city who but a few months ago were in ease and affluence, have now no other alternative than to stay and starve, or turn out to beg.

candidate_claims.statement: The inhabitants of that unfortunate city who but a few months ago were in ease and affluence, have now no other alternative than to stay and starve, or turn out to beg.

evidence.claim: Endangered by the fire of their friends if they continue within the city and plundered by the soldiery if they leave it, in their present situation they are prisoners without the hope of redemption, and in a general attack for their relief they would be exposed to the fury of both armies.

candidate_claims.statement: Endangered by the fire of their friends if they continue within the city and plundered by the soldiery if they leave it, in their present situation they are prisoners without the hope of redemption, and in a general attack for their relief they would be exposed to the fury of both armies.

evidence.claim: Men of passive tempers look somewhat lightly over the offences of Great Britain, and, still hoping for the best, are apt to call out, “Come, come, we shall be friends again for all this.” But examine the passions and feelings of mankind: bring the doctrine of reconciliation to the touchstone of nature, and then tell me whether you can hereafter love, honour, and faithfully serve the power that hath carried fire and sword into your land?

candidate_claims.statement: Men of passive tempers look somewhat lightly over the offences of Great Britain, and, still hoping for the best, are apt to call out, “Come, come, we shall be friends again for all this.” But examine the passions and feelings of mankind: bring the doctrine of reconciliation to the touchstone of nature, and then tell me whether you can hereafter love, honour, and faithfully serve the power that hath carried fire and sword into your land?

evidence.claim: If you cannot do all these, then are you only deceiving yourselves, and by your delay bringing ruin upon posterity.

candidate_claims.statement: If you cannot do all these, then are you only deceiving yourselves, and by your delay bringing ruin upon posterity.

evidence.claim: Your future connection with Britain, whom you can neither love nor honour, will be forced and unnatural, and being formed only on the plan of present convenience, will in a little time fall into a relapse more wretched than the first.

candidate_claims.statement: Your future connection with Britain, whom you can neither love nor honour, will be forced and unnatural, and being formed only on the plan of present convenience, will in a little time fall into a relapse more wretched than the first.

evidence.claim: But if you say, you can still pass the violations over, then I ask, hath your house been burnt?

candidate_claims.statement: But if you say, you can still pass the violations over, then I ask, hath your house been burnt?

evidence.claim: Hath your property been destroyed before your face?

candidate_claims.statement: Hath your property been destroyed before your face?

evidence.claim: Are your wife and children destitute of a bed to lie on, or bread to live on?

candidate_claims.statement: Are your wife and children destitute of a bed to lie on, or bread to live on?

evidence.claim: Have you lost a parent or a child by their hands, and yourself the ruined and wretched survivor?

candidate_claims.statement: Have you lost a parent or a child by their hands, and yourself the ruined and wretched survivor?

evidence.claim: If you have not, then are you not a judge of those who have.

candidate_claims.statement: If you have not, then are you not a judge of those who have.

evidence.claim: But if you have, and can still shake hands with the murderers, then are you unworthy the name of husband, father, friend or lover, and whatever may be your rank or title in life, you have the heart of a coward, and the spirit of a sycophant.

candidate_claims.statement: But if you have, and can still shake hands with the murderers, then are you unworthy the name of husband, father, friend or lover, and whatever may be your rank or title in life, you have the heart of a coward, and the spirit of a sycophant.

evidence.claim: This is not inflaming or exaggerating matters, but trying them by those feelings and affections which nature justifies, and without which, we should be incapable of discharging the social duties of life, or enjoying the felicities of it.

candidate_claims.statement: This is not inflaming or exaggerating matters, but trying them by those feelings and affections which nature justifies, and without which, we should be incapable of discharging the social duties of life, or enjoying the felicities of it.

evidence.claim: I mean not to exhibit horror for the purpose of provoking revenge, but to awaken us from fatal and unmanly slumbers, that we may pursue determinately some fixed object.

candidate_claims.statement: I mean not to exhibit horror for the purpose of provoking revenge, but to awaken us from fatal and unmanly slumbers, that we may pursue determinately some fixed object.

evidence.claim: It is not in the power of Britain or of Europe to conquer America, if she do not conquer herself by _delay_ and _timidity_ .

candidate_claims.statement: It is not in the power of Britain or of Europe to conquer America, if she do not conquer herself by _delay_ and _timidity_ .

evidence.claim: The present winter is worth an age if rightly employed, but if lost or neglected, the whole continent will partake of the misfortune; and there is no punishment which that man will not deserve, be he who, or what, or where he will, that may be the means of sacrificing a season so precious and useful.

candidate_claims.statement: The present winter is worth an age if rightly employed, but if lost or neglected, the whole continent will partake of the misfortune; and there is no punishment which that man will not deserve, be he who, or what, or where he will, that may be the means of sacrificing a season so precious and useful.

evidence.claim: It is repugnant to reason, to the universal order of things to all examples from former ages, to suppose, that this continent can longer remain subject to any external power.

candidate_claims.statement: It is repugnant to reason, to the universal order of things to all examples from former ages, to suppose, that this continent can longer remain subject to any external power.

evidence.claim: The most sanguine in Britain does not think so.

candidate_claims.statement: The most sanguine in Britain does not think so.

evidence.claim: The utmost stretch of human wisdom cannot, at this time, compass a plan short of separation, which can promise the continent even a year’s security.

candidate_claims.statement: The utmost stretch of human wisdom cannot, at this time, compass a plan short of separation, which can promise the continent even a year’s security.

evidence.claim: Reconciliation is _now_ a falacious dream.

candidate_claims.statement: Reconciliation is _now_ a falacious dream.

evidence.claim: Nature hath deserted the connexion, and Art cannot supply her place.

candidate_claims.statement: Nature hath deserted the connexion, and Art cannot supply her place.

evidence.claim: For, as Milton wisely expresses, “never can true reconcilement grow where wounds of deadly hate have pierced so deep.”

candidate_claims.statement: For, as Milton wisely expresses, “never can true reconcilement grow where wounds of deadly hate have pierced so deep.”

evidence.claim: Every quiet method for peace hath been ineffectual.

candidate_claims.statement: Every quiet method for peace hath been ineffectual.

evidence.claim: Our prayers have been rejected with disdain; and only tended to convince us, that nothing flatters vanity, or confirms obstinacy in Kings more than repeated petitioning—and noting hath contributed more than that very measure to make the Kings of Europe absolute: Witness Denmark and Sweden.

candidate_claims.statement: Our prayers have been rejected with disdain; and only tended to convince us, that nothing flatters vanity, or confirms obstinacy in Kings more than repeated petitioning—and noting hath contributed more than that very measure to make the Kings of Europe absolute: Witness Denmark and Sweden.

evidence.claim: Wherefore, since nothing but blows will do, for God’s sake, let us come to a final separation, and not leave the next generation to be cutting throats, under the violated unmeaning names of parent and child.

candidate_claims.statement: Wherefore, since nothing but blows will do, for God’s sake, let us come to a final separation, and not leave the next generation to be cutting throats, under the violated unmeaning names of parent and child.

evidence.claim: To say, they will never attempt it again is idle and visionary, we thought so at the repeal of the stamp act, yet a year or two undeceived us; as well may we suppose that nations, which have been once defeated, will never renew the quarrel.

candidate_claims.statement: To say, they will never attempt it again is idle and visionary, we thought so at the repeal of the stamp act, yet a year or two undeceived us; as well may we suppose that nations, which have been once defeated, will never renew the quarrel.

evidence.claim: As to government matters, it is not in the power of Britain to do this continent justice: The business of it will soon be too weighty, and intricate, to be managed with any tolerable degree of convenience, by a power, so distant from us, and so very ignorant of us; for if they cannot conquer us, they cannot govern us.

candidate_claims.statement: As to government matters, it is not in the power of Britain to do this continent justice: The business of it will soon be too weighty, and intricate, to be managed with any tolerable degree of convenience, by a power, so distant from us, and so very ignorant of us; for if they cannot conquer us, they cannot govern us.

evidence.claim: To be always running three or four thousand miles with a tale or a petition, waiting four or five months for an answer, which when obtained requires five or six more to explain it in, will in a few years be looked upon as folly and childishness—There was a time when it was proper, and there is a proper time for it to cease.

candidate_claims.statement: To be always running three or four thousand miles with a tale or a petition, waiting four or five months for an answer, which when obtained requires five or six more to explain it in, will in a few years be looked upon as folly and childishness—There was a time when it was proper, and there is a proper time for it to cease.

evidence.claim: Small islands not capable of protecting themselves, are the proper objects for kingdoms to take under their care; but there is something very absurd, in supposing a continent to be perpetually governed by an island.

candidate_claims.statement: Small islands not capable of protecting themselves, are the proper objects for kingdoms to take under their care; but there is something very absurd, in supposing a continent to be perpetually governed by an island.

evidence.claim: In no instance hath nature made the satellite larger than its primary planet, and as England and America, with respect to each other, reverses the common order of nature, it is evident they belong to different systems: England to Europe, America to itself.

candidate_claims.statement: In no instance hath nature made the satellite larger than its primary planet, and as England and America, with respect to each other, reverses the common order of nature, it is evident they belong to different systems: England to Europe, America to itself.

evidence.claim: I am not induced by motives of pride, party, or resentment to espouse the doctrine of separation and independance; I am clearly, positively, and conscientiously persuaded that it is the true interest of this continent to be so; that every thing short of _that_ is mere patchwork, that it can afford no lasting felicity,—that it is leaving the sword to our children, and shrinking back at a time, when, a little more, a little farther, would have rendered this continent the glory of the earth.

candidate_claims.statement: I am not induced by motives of pride, party, or resentment to espouse the doctrine of separation and independance; I am clearly, positively, and conscientiously persuaded that it is the true interest of this continent to be so; that every thing short of _that_ is mere patchwork, that it can afford no lasting felicity,—that it is leaving the sword to our children, and shrinking back at a time, when, a little more, a little farther, would have rendered this continent the glory of the earth.

evidence.claim: As Britain hath not manifested the least inclination towards a compromise, we may be assured that no terms can be obtained worthy the acceptance of the continent, or any ways equal to the expense of blood and treasure we have been already put to.

candidate_claims.statement: As Britain hath not manifested the least inclination towards a compromise, we may be assured that no terms can be obtained worthy the acceptance of the continent, or any ways equal to the expense of blood and treasure we have been already put to.

evidence.claim: The object, contended for, ought always to bear some just proportion to the expense.

candidate_claims.statement: The object, contended for, ought always to bear some just proportion to the expense.

evidence.claim: The removal of North, or the whole detestable junto, is a matter unworthy the millions we have expended.

candidate_claims.statement: The removal of North, or the whole detestable junto, is a matter unworthy the millions we have expended.

evidence.claim: A temporary stoppage of trade, was an inconvenience, which would have sufficiently ballanced the repeal of all the acts complained of, had such repeals been obtained; but if the whole continent must take up arms, if every man must be a soldier, it is scarcely worth our while to fight against a contemptible ministry only.

candidate_claims.statement: A temporary stoppage of trade, was an inconvenience, which would have sufficiently ballanced the repeal of all the acts complained of, had such repeals been obtained; but if the whole continent must take up arms, if every man must be a soldier, it is scarcely worth our while to fight against a contemptible ministry only.

evidence.claim: Dearly, dearly, do we pay for the repeal of the acts, if that is all we fight for; for in a just estimation, it is as great a folly to pay a Bunker-hill price for law, as for land.

candidate_claims.statement: Dearly, dearly, do we pay for the repeal of the acts, if that is all we fight for; for in a just estimation, it is as great a folly to pay a Bunker-hill price for law, as for land.

evidence.claim: As I have always considered the independancy of this continent, as an event, which sooner or later must arrive, so from the late rapid progress of the continent to maturity, the event could not be far off.

candidate_claims.statement: As I have always considered the independancy of this continent, as an event, which sooner or later must arrive, so from the late rapid progress of the continent to maturity, the event could not be far off.

evidence.claim: Wherefore, on the breaking out of hostilities, it was not worth the while to have disputed a matter, which time would have finally redressed, unless we meant to be in earnest; otherwise, it is like wasting an estate on a suit at law, to regulate the trespasses of a tenant, whose lease is just expiring.

candidate_claims.statement: Wherefore, on the breaking out of hostilities, it was not worth the while to have disputed a matter, which time would have finally redressed, unless we meant to be in earnest; otherwise, it is like wasting an estate on a suit at law, to regulate the trespasses of a tenant, whose lease is just expiring.

evidence.claim: No man was a warmer wisher for reconciliation than myself, before the fatal nineteenth of April 1775, but the moment the event of that day was made known, I rejected the hardened, sullen tempered Pharaoh of England for ever; and disdain the wretch, that with the pretended title of _Father of His People_ , can unfeelingly hear of their slaughter, and composedly sleep with their blood upon his soul.

candidate_claims.statement: No man was a warmer wisher for reconciliation than myself, before the fatal nineteenth of April 1775, but the moment the event of that day was made known, I rejected the hardened, sullen tempered Pharaoh of England for ever; and disdain the wretch, that with the pretended title of _Father of His People_ , can unfeelingly hear of their slaughter, and composedly sleep with their blood upon his soul.

evidence.claim: But admitting that matters were now made up, what would be the event?

candidate_claims.statement: But admitting that matters were now made up, what would be the event?

evidence.claim: I answer, the ruin of the continent.

candidate_claims.statement: I answer, the ruin of the continent.

evidence.claim: And that for several reasons.

candidate_claims.statement: And that for several reasons.

evidence.claim: The powers of governing still remaining in the hands of the king, he will have a negative over the whole legislation of this continent.

candidate_claims.statement: The powers of governing still remaining in the hands of the king, he will have a negative over the whole legislation of this continent.

evidence.claim: And as he hath shewn himself such an inveterate enemy to liberty, and discovered such a thirst for arbitrary power; is he, or is he not, a proper man to say to these colonies, _“You shall make no laws but what I please.”_ And is there any inhabitant in America so ignorant, as not to know, that according to what is called the _present_

candidate_claims.statement: And as he hath shewn himself such an inveterate enemy to liberty, and discovered such a thirst for arbitrary power; is he, or is he not, a proper man to say to these colonies, _“You shall make no laws but what I please.”_ And is there any inhabitant in America so ignorant, as not to know, that according to what is called the _present_

evidence.claim: _constitution_ , that this continent can make no laws but what the king gives it leave to; and is there any man so unwise, as not to see, that (considering what has happened) he will suffer no law to be made here, but such as suit _his_ purpose.

candidate_claims.statement: _constitution_ , that this continent can make no laws but what the king gives it leave to; and is there any man so unwise, as not to see, that (considering what has happened) he will suffer no law to be made here, but such as suit _his_ purpose.

evidence.claim: We may be as effectually enslaved by the want of laws in America, as by submitting to laws made for us in England.

candidate_claims.statement: We may be as effectually enslaved by the want of laws in America, as by submitting to laws made for us in England.

evidence.claim: After matters are made up (as it is called) can there be any doubt, but the whole power of the crown will be exerted, to keep this continent as low and humble as possible?

candidate_claims.statement: After matters are made up (as it is called) can there be any doubt, but the whole power of the crown will be exerted, to keep this continent as low and humble as possible?

evidence.claim: Instead of going forward we shall go backward, or be perpetually quarrelling or ridiculously petitioning.—We are already greater than the king wishes us to be, and will he not hereafter endeavour to make us less?

candidate_claims.statement: Instead of going forward we shall go backward, or be perpetually quarrelling or ridiculously petitioning.—We are already greater than the king wishes us to be, and will he not hereafter endeavour to make us less?

evidence.claim: To bring the matter to one point.

candidate_claims.statement: To bring the matter to one point.

evidence.claim: Is the power who is jealous of our prosperity, a proper power to govern us?

candidate_claims.statement: Is the power who is jealous of our prosperity, a proper power to govern us?

evidence.claim: Whoever says _No_ to this question is an _independant_ , for independancy means no more, than, whether we shall make our own laws, or, whether the king, the greatest enemy this continent hath, or can have, shall tell us, _“there shall be no laws but such as I like.”_

candidate_claims.statement: Whoever says _No_ to this question is an _independant_ , for independancy means no more, than, whether we shall make our own laws, or, whether the king, the greatest enemy this continent hath, or can have, shall tell us, _“there shall be no laws but such as I like.”_

evidence.claim: But the king you will say has a negative in England; the people there can make no laws without his consent.

candidate_claims.statement: But the king you will say has a negative in England; the people there can make no laws without his consent.

evidence.claim: In point of right and good order, there is something very ridiculous, that a youth of twenty-one (which hath often happened) shall say to several millions of people, older and wiser than himself, I forbid this or that act of yours to be law.

candidate_claims.statement: In point of right and good order, there is something very ridiculous, that a youth of twenty-one (which hath often happened) shall say to several millions of people, older and wiser than himself, I forbid this or that act of yours to be law.

evidence.claim: But in this place I decline this sort of reply, though I will never cease to expose the absurdity of it, and only answer, that England being the King’s residence, and America not so, make quite another case.

candidate_claims.statement: But in this place I decline this sort of reply, though I will never cease to expose the absurdity of it, and only answer, that England being the King’s residence, and America not so, make quite another case.

evidence.claim: The king’s negative _here_ is ten times more dangerous and fatal than it can be in England, for _there_ he will scarcely refuse his consent to a bill for putting England into as strong a state of defence as possible, and in America he would never suffer such a bill to be passed.

candidate_claims.statement: The king’s negative _here_ is ten times more dangerous and fatal than it can be in England, for _there_ he will scarcely refuse his consent to a bill for putting England into as strong a state of defence as possible, and in America he would never suffer such a bill to be passed.

evidence.claim: America is only a secondary object in the system of British politics, England consults the good of _this_ country, no farther than it answers her _own_ purpose.

candidate_claims.statement: America is only a secondary object in the system of British politics, England consults the good of _this_ country, no farther than it answers her _own_ purpose.

evidence.claim: Wherefore, her own interest leads her to suppress the growth of _ours_ in every case which doth not promote her advantage, or in the least interferes with it.

candidate_claims.statement: Wherefore, her own interest leads her to suppress the growth of _ours_ in every case which doth not promote her advantage, or in the least interferes with it.

evidence.claim: A pretty state we should soon be in under such a second-hand government, considering what has happened!

candidate_claims.statement: A pretty state we should soon be in under such a second-hand government, considering what has happened!

evidence.claim: Men do not change from enemies to friends by the alteration of a name: And in order to shew that reconciliation _now_ is a dangerous doctrine, I affirm, _that it would be policy in the king at this time, to repeal the acts for the sake of reinstating himself in the government of the provinces_ ; in order that _he may accomplish by craft and subtility, in the long run, what he cannot do by force and violence in the short one_ .

candidate_claims.statement: Men do not change from enemies to friends by the alteration of a name: And in order to shew that reconciliation _now_ is a dangerous doctrine, I affirm, _that it would be policy in the king at this time, to repeal the acts for the sake of reinstating himself in the government of the provinces_ ; in order that _he may accomplish by craft and subtility, in the long run, what he cannot do by force and violence in the short one_ .

evidence.claim: Reconciliation and ruin are nearly related.

candidate_claims.statement: Reconciliation and ruin are nearly related.

evidence.claim: That as even the best terms, which we can expect to obtain, can amount to no more than a temporary expedient, or a kind of government by guardianship, which can last no longer than till the colonies come of age, so the general face and state of things, in the interim, will be unsettled and unpromising.

candidate_claims.statement: That as even the best terms, which we can expect to obtain, can amount to no more than a temporary expedient, or a kind of government by guardianship, which can last no longer than till the colonies come of age, so the general face and state of things, in the interim, will be unsettled and unpromising.

evidence.claim: Emigrants of property will not choose to come to a country whose form of government hangs but by a thread, and who is every day tottering on the brink of commotion and disturbance; and numbers of the present inhabitants would lay hold of the interval, to dispose of their effects, and quit the continent.

candidate_claims.statement: Emigrants of property will not choose to come to a country whose form of government hangs but by a thread, and who is every day tottering on the brink of commotion and disturbance; and numbers of the present inhabitants would lay hold of the interval, to dispose of their effects, and quit the continent.

evidence.claim: But the most powerful of all arguments, is, that nothing but independance, i.

candidate_claims.statement: But the most powerful of all arguments, is, that nothing but independance, i.

evidence.claim: a continental form of government, can keep the peace of the continent and preserve it inviolate from civil wars.

candidate_claims.statement: a continental form of government, can keep the peace of the continent and preserve it inviolate from civil wars.

evidence.claim: I dread the event of a reconciliation with Britain now, as it is more than probable, that it will followed by a revolt somewhere or other, the consequences of which may be far more fatal than all the malice of Britain.

candidate_claims.statement: I dread the event of a reconciliation with Britain now, as it is more than probable, that it will followed by a revolt somewhere or other, the consequences of which may be far more fatal than all the malice of Britain.

evidence.claim: Thousands are already ruined by British barbarity; (thousands more will probably suffer the same fate.) Those men have other feelings than us who have nothing suffered.

candidate_claims.statement: Thousands are already ruined by British barbarity; (thousands more will probably suffer the same fate.) Those men have other feelings than us who have nothing suffered.

evidence.claim: All they _now_ possess is liberty, what they before enjoyed is sacrificed to its service, and having nothing more to lose, they disdain submission.

candidate_claims.statement: All they _now_ possess is liberty, what they before enjoyed is sacrificed to its service, and having nothing more to lose, they disdain submission.

evidence.claim: Besides, the general temper of the colonies, towards a British government, will be like that of a youth, who is nearly out of his time; they will care very little about her.

candidate_claims.statement: Besides, the general temper of the colonies, towards a British government, will be like that of a youth, who is nearly out of his time; they will care very little about her.

evidence.claim: And a government which cannot preserve the peace, is no government at all, and in that case we pay our money for nothing; and pray what is it that Britain can do, whose power will be wholly on paper, should a civil tumult break out the very day after

candidate_claims.statement: And a government which cannot preserve the peace, is no government at all, and in that case we pay our money for nothing; and pray what is it that Britain can do, whose power will be wholly on paper, should a civil tumult break out the very day after

evidence.claim: I have heard some men say, many of whom I believe spoke without thinking, that they dreaded an independance, fearing that it would produce civil wars.

candidate_claims.statement: I have heard some men say, many of whom I believe spoke without thinking, that they dreaded an independance, fearing that it would produce civil wars.

evidence.claim: It is but seldom that our first thoughts are truly correct, and that is the case here; for there are ten times more to dread from a patched up connexion than from independance.

candidate_claims.statement: It is but seldom that our first thoughts are truly correct, and that is the case here; for there are ten times more to dread from a patched up connexion than from independance.

evidence.claim: I make the sufferers case my own, and I protest, that were I driven from house and home, my property destroyed, and my circumstances ruined, that as a man, sensible of injuries, I could never relish the doctrine of reconciliation, or consider myself bound thereby.

candidate_claims.statement: I make the sufferers case my own, and I protest, that were I driven from house and home, my property destroyed, and my circumstances ruined, that as a man, sensible of injuries, I could never relish the doctrine of reconciliation, or consider myself bound thereby.

evidence.claim: The colonies have manifested such a spirit of good order and obedience to continental government, as is sufficient to make every reasonable person easy and happy on that head.

candidate_claims.statement: The colonies have manifested such a spirit of good order and obedience to continental government, as is sufficient to make every reasonable person easy and happy on that head.

evidence.claim: No man can assign the least pretence for his fears, on any other grounds, that such as are truly childish and ridiculous, viz.

candidate_claims.statement: No man can assign the least pretence for his fears, on any other grounds, that such as are truly childish and ridiculous, viz.

evidence.claim: that one colony will be striving for superiority over another.

candidate_claims.statement: that one colony will be striving for superiority over another.

evidence.claim: Where there are no distinctions there can be no superiority, perfect equality affords no temptation.

candidate_claims.statement: Where there are no distinctions there can be no superiority, perfect equality affords no temptation.

evidence.claim: The republics of Europe are all (and we may say always) in peace.

candidate_claims.statement: The republics of Europe are all (and we may say always) in peace.

evidence.claim: Holland and Swisserland are without wars, foreign or domestic: Monarchical governments, it is true, are never long at rest; the crown itself is a temptation to enterprizing ruffians at _home_ ; and that degree of pride and insolence ever attendant on regal authority, swells into a rupture with foreign powers, in instances where a republican government, by being formed on more natural principles, would negotiate the mistake.

candidate_claims.statement: Holland and Swisserland are without wars, foreign or domestic: Monarchical governments, it is true, are never long at rest; the crown itself is a temptation to enterprizing ruffians at _home_ ; and that degree of pride and insolence ever attendant on regal authority, swells into a rupture with foreign powers, in instances where a republican government, by being formed on more natural principles, would negotiate the mistake.

evidence.claim: If there is any true cause of fear respecting independance, it is because no plan is yet laid down.

candidate_claims.statement: If there is any true cause of fear respecting independance, it is because no plan is yet laid down.

evidence.claim: Men do not see their way out—Wherefore, as an opening into that business, I offer the following hints; at the same time modestly affirming, that I have no other opinion of them myself, than that they may be the means of giving rise to something better.

candidate_claims.statement: Men do not see their way out—Wherefore, as an opening into that business, I offer the following hints; at the same time modestly affirming, that I have no other opinion of them myself, than that they may be the means of giving rise to something better.

evidence.claim: Could the straggling thoughts of individuals be collected, they would frequently form materials for wise and able men to improve into useful matter.

candidate_claims.statement: Could the straggling thoughts of individuals be collected, they would frequently form materials for wise and able men to improve into useful matter.

evidence.claim: Let the assemblies be annual, with a President only.

candidate_claims.statement: Let the assemblies be annual, with a President only.

evidence.claim: The representation more equal.

candidate_claims.statement: The representation more equal.

evidence.claim: Their business wholly domestic, and subject to the authority of a Continental Congress.

candidate_claims.statement: Their business wholly domestic, and subject to the authority of a Continental Congress.

evidence.claim: Let each colony be divided into six, eight, or ten, convenient districts, each district to send a proper number of delegates to Congress, so that each colony send at least thirty.

candidate_claims.statement: Let each colony be divided into six, eight, or ten, convenient districts, each district to send a proper number of delegates to Congress, so that each colony send at least thirty.

evidence.claim: The whole number in Congress will be least 390.

candidate_claims.statement: The whole number in Congress will be least 390.

evidence.claim: Each Congress to sit and to choose a president by the following method.

candidate_claims.statement: Each Congress to sit and to choose a president by the following method.

evidence.claim: When the delegates are met, let a colony be taken from the whole thirteen colonies by lot, after which, let the whole Congress choose (by ballot) a president from out of the delegates of _that_ province.

candidate_claims.statement: When the delegates are met, let a colony be taken from the whole thirteen colonies by lot, after which, let the whole Congress choose (by ballot) a president from out of the delegates of _that_ province.

evidence.claim: In the next Congress, let a colony be taken by lot from twelve only, omitting that colony from which the president was taken in the former Congress, and so proceeding on till the whole thirteen shall have had their proper rotation.

candidate_claims.statement: In the next Congress, let a colony be taken by lot from twelve only, omitting that colony from which the president was taken in the former Congress, and so proceeding on till the whole thirteen shall have had their proper rotation.

evidence.claim: And in order that nothing may pass into a law but what is satisfactorily just, not less than three fifths of the Congress to be called a majority.—He that will promote discord, under a government so equally formed as this, would have joined Lucifer in his revolt.

candidate_claims.statement: And in order that nothing may pass into a law but what is satisfactorily just, not less than three fifths of the Congress to be called a majority.—He that will promote discord, under a government so equally formed as this, would have joined Lucifer in his revolt.

evidence.claim: But as there is a peculiar delicacy, from whom, or in what manner, this business must first arise, and as it seems most agreeable and consistent that it should come from some intermediate body between the governed and the governors, that is, between the Congress and the people, let a _continental conference_ be held, in the following manner, and for the following purpose.
