evidence.claim: that had this rupture happened forty or fifty years hence, instead of now, the continent would have been more able to have shaken off the dependence.

source_claims.statement: that had this rupture happened forty or fifty years hence, instead of now, the continent would have been more able to have shaken off the dependence.

evidence.claim: To which I reply, that our military ability, at this time, arises from the experience gained in the last war, and which in forty or fifty years’ time, would be totally extinct.

source_claims.statement: To which I reply, that our military ability, at this time, arises from the experience gained in the last war, and which in forty or fifty years’ time, would be totally extinct.

evidence.claim: The continent would not, by that time, have a quitrent reserved thereon will always lessen, and in time will wholly support, the yearly expense of government.

source_claims.statement: The continent would not, by that time, have a quitrent reserved thereon will always lessen, and in time will wholly support, the yearly expense of government.

evidence.claim: It matters not how long the debt is in paying, so that the lands when sold be applied to the discharge of it, and for the execution of which the Congress for the time being will be the continental trustees.

source_claims.statement: It matters not how long the debt is in paying, so that the lands when sold be applied to the discharge of it, and for the execution of which the Congress for the time being will be the continental trustees.

evidence.claim: I proceed now to the second head, viz.

source_claims.statement: I proceed now to the second head, viz.

evidence.claim: Which is the easiest and most practicable plan, reconciliation or independence; with some occasional remarks.

source_claims.statement: Which is the easiest and most practicable plan, reconciliation or independence; with some occasional remarks.

evidence.claim: He who takes nature for his guide, is not easily beaten out of his argument, and on that ground, I answer generally that independence being a single simple line, contained within ourselves; and reconciliation, a matter exceedingly perplexed and complicated, and in which a treacherous capricious court is to interfere, gives the answer without a doubt.

source_claims.statement: He who takes nature for his guide, is not easily beaten out of his argument, and on that ground, I answer generally that independence being a single simple line, contained within ourselves; and reconciliation, a matter exceedingly perplexed and complicated, and in which a treacherous capricious court is to interfere, gives the answer without a doubt.

evidence.claim: The present state of America is truly alarming to every man who is capable of reflection.

source_claims.statement: The present state of America is truly alarming to every man who is capable of reflection.

evidence.claim: Without law, without government, without any other mode of power than what is founded on, and granted by, courtesy.

source_claims.statement: Without law, without government, without any other mode of power than what is founded on, and granted by, courtesy.

evidence.claim: Held together by an unexampled occurrence of sentiment, which is nevertheless subject to change, and which every secret enemy is endeavoring to dissolve.

source_claims.statement: Held together by an unexampled occurrence of sentiment, which is nevertheless subject to change, and which every secret enemy is endeavoring to dissolve.

evidence.claim: Our present condition is, Legislation without law; wisdom without a plan; a constitution without a name; and, what is strangely astonishing, perfect independence contending for dependence.

source_claims.statement: Our present condition is, Legislation without law; wisdom without a plan; a constitution without a name; and, what is strangely astonishing, perfect independence contending for dependence.

evidence.claim: The instance is without a precedent, the case never existed before, and who can tell what may be the event?

source_claims.statement: The instance is without a precedent, the case never existed before, and who can tell what may be the event?

evidence.claim: The property of no man is secure in the present un-braced system of things.

source_claims.statement: The property of no man is secure in the present un-braced system of things.

evidence.claim: The mind of the multitude is left at random, and seeing no fixed object before them, they pursue such as fancy or opinion presents.

source_claims.statement: The mind of the multitude is left at random, and seeing no fixed object before them, they pursue such as fancy or opinion presents.

evidence.claim: Nothing is criminal; there is no such thing as treason wherefore, every one thinks himself at liberty to act as he pleases.

source_claims.statement: Nothing is criminal; there is no such thing as treason wherefore, every one thinks himself at liberty to act as he pleases.

evidence.claim: The Tories would not have dared to assemble offensively, had they known that their lives, by that act, were forfeited to the laws of the state.

source_claims.statement: The Tories would not have dared to assemble offensively, had they known that their lives, by that act, were forfeited to the laws of the state.

evidence.claim: A line of distinction should be drawn between English soldiers taken in battle, and inhabitants of America taken in arms.

source_claims.statement: A line of distinction should be drawn between English soldiers taken in battle, and inhabitants of America taken in arms.

evidence.claim: The first are prisoners, but the latter traitors.

source_claims.statement: The first are prisoners, but the latter traitors.

evidence.claim: The one forfeits his liberty, the other his head.

source_claims.statement: The one forfeits his liberty, the other his head.

evidence.claim: Notwithstanding our wisdom, there is a visible feebleness in some of our proceedings which gives encouragement to dissensions.

source_claims.statement: Notwithstanding our wisdom, there is a visible feebleness in some of our proceedings which gives encouragement to dissensions.

evidence.claim: The continental belt is too loosely buckled: And if something is not done in time, it will be too late to do any thing, and we shall fall into a state, in which neither reconciliation nor independence will be practicable.

source_claims.statement: The continental belt is too loosely buckled: And if something is not done in time, it will be too late to do any thing, and we shall fall into a state, in which neither reconciliation nor independence will be practicable.

evidence.claim: The king and his worthless adherents are got at their old game of dividing the continent, and there are not wanting among us printers who will be busy in spreading specious falsehoods.

source_claims.statement: The king and his worthless adherents are got at their old game of dividing the continent, and there are not wanting among us printers who will be busy in spreading specious falsehoods.

evidence.claim: The artful and hypocritical letter which appeared a few months ago in two of the New York papers, and likewise in two others, is an evidence that there are men who want both judgment and honesty.

source_claims.statement: The artful and hypocritical letter which appeared a few months ago in two of the New York papers, and likewise in two others, is an evidence that there are men who want both judgment and honesty.

evidence.claim: It is easy getting into holes and corners, and talking of reconciliation: But do such men seriously consider how difficult the task is, and how dangerous it may prove, should the continent divide thereon?

source_claims.statement: It is easy getting into holes and corners, and talking of reconciliation: But do such men seriously consider how difficult the task is, and how dangerous it may prove, should the continent divide thereon?

evidence.claim: Do they take within their view all the various orders of men whose situation and circumstances, as well as their own, are to be considered therein?

source_claims.statement: Do they take within their view all the various orders of men whose situation and circumstances, as well as their own, are to be considered therein?

evidence.claim: Do they put themselves in the place of the sufferer whose all is already gone, and of the soldier, who hath quitted all for the defence of his country?

source_claims.statement: Do they put themselves in the place of the sufferer whose all is already gone, and of the soldier, who hath quitted all for the defence of his country?

evidence.claim: If their ill-judged moderation be suited to their own private situations only, regardless of others, the event will convince them that “they are reckoning without their host.”

source_claims.statement: If their ill-judged moderation be suited to their own private situations only, regardless of others, the event will convince them that “they are reckoning without their host.”

evidence.claim: Put us, say some, on the footing we were in the year 1763: To which I answer, the request is not now in the power of Britain to comply with, neither will she propose it; but if it were, and even should be granted, I ask, as a reasonable question, By what means is such a corrupt and faithless court to be kept to its engagements?

source_claims.statement: Put us, say some, on the footing we were in the year 1763: To which I answer, the request is not now in the power of Britain to comply with, neither will she propose it; but if it were, and even should be granted, I ask, as a reasonable question, By what means is such a corrupt and faithless court to be kept to its engagements?

evidence.claim: Another parliament, nay, even the present, may hereafter repeal the obligation, on the pretence of its being violently obtained, or unit wisely granted; and, in that case, Where is our redress?

source_claims.statement: Another parliament, nay, even the present, may hereafter repeal the obligation, on the pretence of its being violently obtained, or unit wisely granted; and, in that case, Where is our redress?

evidence.claim: No going to law with nations; cannon are the barristers of crowns; and the sword, not of justice, but of war, decides the suit.

source_claims.statement: No going to law with nations; cannon are the barristers of crowns; and the sword, not of justice, but of war, decides the suit.

evidence.claim: To be on the footing of 1763, it is not sufficient, that the laws only be put in the same state, but, that our circumstances likewise be put in the same state; our burnt and destroyed towns repaired or built up, our private losses made good, our public debts (contracted for defence) discharged; otherwise we shall be millions worse than we were at that enviable period.

source_claims.statement: To be on the footing of 1763, it is not sufficient, that the laws only be put in the same state, but, that our circumstances likewise be put in the same state; our burnt and destroyed towns repaired or built up, our private losses made good, our public debts (contracted for defence) discharged; otherwise we shall be millions worse than we were at that enviable period.

evidence.claim: Such a request, had it been complied with a year ago, would have won the heart and soul of the continent, but now it is too late.

source_claims.statement: Such a request, had it been complied with a year ago, would have won the heart and soul of the continent, but now it is too late.

evidence.claim: “The Rubicon is passed.” Besides, the taking up arms, merely to enforce the repeal of a pecuniary law, seems as unwarrantable by the divine law, and as repugnant to human feelings, as the taking up arms to enforce obedience thereto.

source_claims.statement: “The Rubicon is passed.” Besides, the taking up arms, merely to enforce the repeal of a pecuniary law, seems as unwarrantable by the divine law, and as repugnant to human feelings, as the taking up arms to enforce obedience thereto.

evidence.claim: The object, on either side, doth not justify the means; for the lives of men are too valuable to be cast away on such trifles.

source_claims.statement: The object, on either side, doth not justify the means; for the lives of men are too valuable to be cast away on such trifles.

evidence.claim: It is the violence which is done and threatened to our persons; the destruction of our property by an armed force; the invasion of our country by fire and sword, which conscientiously qualifies the use of arms: and the instant in which such mode of defence became necessary, all subjection to Britain ought to have ceased; and the independence of America should have been considered as dating its era from, and published by, the first musket that was fired against her.

source_claims.statement: It is the violence which is done and threatened to our persons; the destruction of our property by an armed force; the invasion of our country by fire and sword, which conscientiously qualifies the use of arms: and the instant in which such mode of defence became necessary, all subjection to Britain ought to have ceased; and the independence of America should have been considered as dating its era from, and published by, the first musket that was fired against her.

evidence.claim: This line is a line of consistency; neither drawn by caprice, nor extended by ambition; but produced by a chain of events, of which the colonies were not the authors.

source_claims.statement: This line is a line of consistency; neither drawn by caprice, nor extended by ambition; but produced by a chain of events, of which the colonies were not the authors.

evidence.claim: I shall conclude these remarks, with the following timely and well-intended hints.

source_claims.statement: I shall conclude these remarks, with the following timely and well-intended hints.

evidence.claim: We ought to reflect, that there are three different ways by which an independency may hereafter be effected, and that one of those three, will, one day or other, be the fate of America, viz.

source_claims.statement: We ought to reflect, that there are three different ways by which an independency may hereafter be effected, and that one of those three, will, one day or other, be the fate of America, viz.

evidence.claim: By the legal voice of the people in Congress; by a military power, or by a mob: It may not always happen that our soldiers are citizens, and the multitude a body of reasonable men; virtue, as I have already remarked, is not hereditary, neither is it perpetual.

source_claims.statement: By the legal voice of the people in Congress; by a military power, or by a mob: It may not always happen that our soldiers are citizens, and the multitude a body of reasonable men; virtue, as I have already remarked, is not hereditary, neither is it perpetual.

evidence.claim: Should an independency be brought about by the first of those means, we have every opportunity and every encouragement before us, to form the noblest, purest constitution on the face of the earth.

source_claims.statement: Should an independency be brought about by the first of those means, we have every opportunity and every encouragement before us, to form the noblest, purest constitution on the face of the earth.

evidence.claim: We have it in our power to begin the world over again.

source_claims.statement: We have it in our power to begin the world over again.

evidence.claim: A situation, similar to the present, hath not happened since the days of Noah until now.

source_claims.statement: A situation, similar to the present, hath not happened since the days of Noah until now.

evidence.claim: The birthday of a new world is at hand, and a race of men, perhaps as numerous as all Europe contains, are to receive their portion of freedom from the events of a few months.

source_claims.statement: The birthday of a new world is at hand, and a race of men, perhaps as numerous as all Europe contains, are to receive their portion of freedom from the events of a few months.

evidence.claim: The reflection is awful, and in this point of view, how trifling, how ridiculous, do the little paltry cavilings of a few weak or interested men appear, when weighed against the business of a world.

source_claims.statement: The reflection is awful, and in this point of view, how trifling, how ridiculous, do the little paltry cavilings of a few weak or interested men appear, when weighed against the business of a world.

evidence.claim: Should we neglect the present favorable and inviting period, and independence be hereafter effected by any other means, we must charge the consequence to ourselves, or to those rather whose narrow and prejudiced souls are habitually opposing the measure, without either inquiring or reflecting.

source_claims.statement: Should we neglect the present favorable and inviting period, and independence be hereafter effected by any other means, we must charge the consequence to ourselves, or to those rather whose narrow and prejudiced souls are habitually opposing the measure, without either inquiring or reflecting.

evidence.claim: There are reasons to be given in support of independence which men should rather privately think of, than be publicly told of.

source_claims.statement: There are reasons to be given in support of independence which men should rather privately think of, than be publicly told of.

evidence.claim: We ought not now to be debating whether we shall be independent or not, but anxious to accomplish it on a firm, secure, and honorable basis, and uneasy rather that it is not yet began upon.

source_claims.statement: We ought not now to be debating whether we shall be independent or not, but anxious to accomplish it on a firm, secure, and honorable basis, and uneasy rather that it is not yet began upon.

evidence.claim: Even the Tories (if such beings yet remain among us) should, of all men, be the most solicitous to promote it; for as the appointment of committees at first protected them from popular rage, so, a wise and well established form of government will be the only certain means of continuing it securely to them.

source_claims.statement: Even the Tories (if such beings yet remain among us) should, of all men, be the most solicitous to promote it; for as the appointment of committees at first protected them from popular rage, so, a wise and well established form of government will be the only certain means of continuing it securely to them.

evidence.claim: Wherefore, if they have not virtue enough to be _Whigs_ , they ought to have prudence enough to wish for independence.

source_claims.statement: Wherefore, if they have not virtue enough to be _Whigs_ , they ought to have prudence enough to wish for independence.

evidence.claim: In short, independence is the only bond that tie and keep us together.

source_claims.statement: In short, independence is the only bond that tie and keep us together.

evidence.claim: We shall then see our object, and our ears will be legally shut against the schemes of an intriguing, as well as cruel, enemy.

source_claims.statement: We shall then see our object, and our ears will be legally shut against the schemes of an intriguing, as well as cruel, enemy.

evidence.claim: We shall then, too, be on a proper footing to treat with Britain; for there is reason to conclude, that the pride of that court will be less hurt by treating with the American States for terms of peace, than with those, whom she denominates “rebellious subjects,” for terms of accommodation.

source_claims.statement: We shall then, too, be on a proper footing to treat with Britain; for there is reason to conclude, that the pride of that court will be less hurt by treating with the American States for terms of peace, than with those, whom she denominates “rebellious subjects,” for terms of accommodation.

evidence.claim: It is our delaying in that, encourages her to hope for conquest, and our backwardness tends only to prolong the war.

source_claims.statement: It is our delaying in that, encourages her to hope for conquest, and our backwardness tends only to prolong the war.

evidence.claim: As we have, without any good effect therefrom, withheld our trade to obtain a redress of our grievances, let us now try the alternative, by independently redressing them ourselves, and then offering to open the trade.

source_claims.statement: As we have, without any good effect therefrom, withheld our trade to obtain a redress of our grievances, let us now try the alternative, by independently redressing them ourselves, and then offering to open the trade.

evidence.claim: The mercantile and reasonable part of England, will be still with us; because, peace, with trade, is preferable to war without it.

source_claims.statement: The mercantile and reasonable part of England, will be still with us; because, peace, with trade, is preferable to war without it.

evidence.claim: And if this offer be not accepted, other courts may be applied to.

source_claims.statement: And if this offer be not accepted, other courts may be applied to.

evidence.claim: On these grounds I rest the matter.

source_claims.statement: On these grounds I rest the matter.

evidence.claim: And as no offer hath yet been made to refute the doctrine contained in the former editions of this pamphlet, it is a negative proof, that either the doctrine cannot be refuted, or, that the party in favor of it are too numerous to be opposed.

source_claims.statement: And as no offer hath yet been made to refute the doctrine contained in the former editions of this pamphlet, it is a negative proof, that either the doctrine cannot be refuted, or, that the party in favor of it are too numerous to be opposed.

evidence.claim: _wherefore_ , instead of gazing at each other with suspicious or doubtful curiosity, let each of us hold out to his neighbor the hearty hand of friendship, and unite in drawing a line, which, like an act of oblivion, shall bury in forgetfulness every former dissension.

evidence.claim: Let the names of Whig and Tory be extinct; and let none other be heard among us, than those of a good citizen, an open and resolute friend, and a virtuous supporter of the _rights of mankind_ , and of the _free and independent States of America_ .

source_claims.statement: Let the names of Whig and Tory be extinct; and let none other be heard among us, than those of a good citizen, an open and resolute friend, and a virtuous supporter of the _rights of mankind_ , and of the _free and independent States of America_ .
